This is an opportunity to join Ethos Specialty, a Bishop Street Underwriters Company Ethos Specialty Insurance Services is a specialty Managing General Underwriter (MGU) that partners with carriers to underwrite tailored insurance programs across North America. Ethos benefits from deep capabilities and underwriting expertise and is able to match risk to multiple sources of capital as well providing the highest quality, independent underwriting service to its industry partners and capital providers. Ethos currently has three programs under the M&A insurance space. The Tax Underwriting Team – What do we do? Corporate taxpayers in North America are generally required to take positions on certain tax issues they encounter during the course of their business based on their understanding of the facts and law. This could be in the context of a transaction such as a merger or acquisition being undertaken, tax credits being generated or utilized, or upon an internal review and as part of the taxpayer's risk mitigation strategy. The taxing authorities in the relevant jurisdiction may agree or disagree with the position the taxpayer has taken. If the taxing authority disagrees with a certain tax position, the taxpayer could be assessed for additional taxes, interest and penalties as a result. The Ethos Tax underwriting team, upon completion of an in-depth underwriting process, provides tax liability insurance to mitigate the risk of an adverse determination by the taxing authority. Amongst other benefits, tax liability insurance simplifies otherwise complex negotiations during a transaction by ringfencing certain tax issues and more or less allows a taxpayer to transfer a potential tax liability off its balance sheet. Job Purpose: The Analyst will join Ethos and will be primarily responsible for assisting the Tax Team/underwriters in coordinating the underwriting process and assisting in providing client solutions. Among other things, the Analyst will: Coordinate with external legal counsel as required and develop strong working relationships with M&A/Tax insurance brokers, attorneys and deal principals. Collect and analyze data related to submissions, bound deals, and policies Draft invoices, expense agreements, non-binding indication letters and other documents, as requested by underwriters Review underwriting submissions and files and summarize key points for review and discussion with underwriters on the team Assist in the underwriting process for tax liability insurance products by collaborating with various brokers, advisors and law firms Assess and execute Non-Disclosure Agreements, Non-Reliance Letters and Access letters to obtain access to proprietary deal information Attend due diligence calls to transcribe accurate and comprehensive notes Competencies/Requirements: 1 - 2 years’ experience in a related field (i.e., insurance, banking, accounting) Bachelor's degree required Experience handling and interpreting large sets of data Strong written and oral communication skills Strong interpersonal skills and ability to collaborate with a team Ability to adapt to a fast-paced environment, including working under short timelines, and occasionally during off-hours Detail-oriented Ability to work independently Technical Skills & Knowledge:Strong proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, Outlook, PowerPoint, Teams), Adobe Acrobat

Company Description: ProntoPagaJob Description: ¡Súmate a ProntoPaga!Somos una Fintech en expansión que transforma la manera en que se gestionan los pagos en Latinoamérica. Creemos en la innovación, la colaboración y el talento sin fronteras.Nuestra misión es simplificar lo complejo y construir un ecosistema financiero más accesible y seguro.Buscamos personas apasionadas, creativas y con ganas de crecer junto a un equipo multidisciplinario y comprometido.Aquí valoramos la diversidad, la colaboración y el deseo constante de superarse.Si te motiva trabajar en un entorno dinámico, inclusivo y con impacto real… ¡Este es tu lugar!Buscamos a un profesional para apoyar la gestión operativa y administrativa del área de Compensaciones y Beneficios, entregando soporte directo a la Jefatura y al rol Senior del área, asegurando la correcta ejecución de los procesos de remuneraciones, beneficios, control documental y atención a colaboradores, contribuyendo al orden, cumplimiento y continuidad operativa del área.¿Qué funciones realizarás? Soporte a Compensaciones: Apoyar el proceso mensual de remuneraciones, recopilando y validando información base (novedades, bonos, descuentos, incidencias). Apoyar en la revisión de liquidaciones de sueldo, identificando inconsistencias y levantándolas a la Jefatura o Analista Senior. Apoyo en Beneficios Gestionar la administración operativa de beneficios (seguro complementario, convenios, caja de compensación, bienestar, etc.). Apoyar la incorporación y baja de colaboradores en beneficios corporativos. Resolver consultas de colaboradores relacionadas con beneficios, escalando los casos que correspondan. Apoyar en la comunicación interna de beneficios (levantamiento de información, envío de comunicaciones, apoyo en campañas). Gestión administrativa y documental Confeccionar y mantener ordenada la documentación laboral asociada al área (contratos, anexos, cartas, respaldos). Apoyar en la carga y actualización de información en plataformas externas (Dirección del Trabajo, Previred u otras, según corresponda). Mantener archivos digitales y físicos actualizados, asegurando trazabilidad y respaldo de la información. Apoyar auditorías internas y externas, recopilando antecedentes solicitados. Atención a colaboradores Ser primer punto de contacto para consultas operativas de C&B (liquidaciones, descuentos, beneficios, certificados). Derivar oportunamente consultas de mayor complejidad a la Jefatura o Analista Senior. Entregar orientación clara y oportuna, manteniendo un trato respetuoso y confidencial. Apoyo transversal al equipo Brindar apoyo directo y permanente tanto a la Jefatura como al rol Senior del área, priorizando tareas según lineamientos definidos. Colaborar en proyectos de ordenamiento, mejora de procesos y estandarización del área. Apoyar en levantamiento de información y elaboración de reportes básicos del área.¿Cuáles son los requisitos?Formación académica Técnico en Administración de Empresas, Recursos Humanos, Contabilidad o carrera afín. Deseable formación o cursos en remuneraciones y legislación laboral.Experiencia Al menos 1 a 3 años de experiencia en áreas de remuneraciones, compensaciones y/o gestión de personas. Experiencia previa en tareas administrativas y de soporte en RRHH.Conocimientos técnicos Conocimientos básicos de legislación laboral chilena. Manejo de procesos de remuneraciones y beneficios. Manejo intermedio de Excel. Experiencia o conocimiento en sistemas de RRHH (ideal BUK u otro similar).¿Qué Ofrecemos? Bono de cumpleaños, fiestas patrias y navidad. Acceso a nuestro palco VIP en Movistar Arena como beneficio adicional de cumpleaños. Seguro Complementario de Salud. Convenio Colectivo FALP. Ambiente de apoyo y crecimiento.Compromiso con la Diversidad e InclusiónEn ProntoPaga promovemos un ambiente laboral diverso, equitativo e inclusivo. Valoramos las distintas perspectivas y experiencias, y creemos que estas enriquecen nuestro equipo. Todas las personas son bienvenidas a postular, sin distinción de género, orientación sexual, edad, nacionalidad, origen étnico, religión o discapacidad.

About Inspire Brands:Inspire Brands is disrupting the restaurant industry through digital transformation and operational efficiencies. The company’s technology hub, Inspire Brands Hyderabad Support Center, India, will lead technology innovation and product development for the organization and its portfolio of distinct brands. The Inspire Brands Hyderabad Support Center will focus on developing new capabilities in data science, data analytics, eCommerce, automation, cloud computing, and information security to accelerate the company’s business strategy. This role is highly collaborative and highly visible given the lease accounting risk to the financial statements and important stakeholders (lease admin / legal, accounts payable, financial reporting, corporate accounting, FP&A, Treasury, Tax, etc.).Additionally, this role will partner with other functional teams, creating efficiency, automation, and standardization over the end-to-end process.RESPONSIBILITIES:Prepare monthly base / fixed rent payments and billings for all brands via Costar.Prepare variable rent calculation reports, submit to Lease Management for approval and process payments via Costar.Prepare miscellaneous charges for payment / billing on a weekly / bi-weekly basis via Costar.Support lease accounting process by maintaining proper accounting of activity in accordance with GAAP and in compliance with internal and external audits.Liaise with other departments within the organization to ensure lease transactions are properly accounted.Complete month-end close processes including subledger work in CoStar, general ledger adjustment and accrual entries, and internal financial reporting deliverables, including roll forwards, footnotes, and controls.Prepare and / or review month-end journal entries and perform reconciliations and analysis of assigned accounts.Perform ad-hoc projects to help analyze or support Company decisions.EXPERIENCE AND QUALIFICATIONS:Bachelor’s (or equivalent) degree in accounting required.Chartered Accountant preferred.Minimum 5 years of public accounting or large multinational company accounting experience (property accounting preferred).Experience with general ledger accounting systems (Oracle Cloud preferred).Proficient understanding of US GAAP-based financial accounting (ASC 842, Lease Accounting, experience preferred).REQUIRED KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS, OR ABILITIES:Flexible, “hands-on”, self-starter with strong initiative and work ethic.Business-minded, analytical accountant who can work through complex technical accounting issues while balancing and managing details as needed.Ability to work in a dynamic, fast paced, fast growing company.Ability to develop effective workplans and meet assigned deadlines.Team player who works with appropriate sense of urgency and ownership to meet deadlines and make progress on multiple brands, projects, and workstreams simultaneously.Excellent communication skills and a customer service.Proficient in MS Excel (e.g. pivot tables, X-lookups, etc.) and Word.Oracle knowledge is a plus.Inspire Brands is a multi-brand restaurant company whose portfolio includes nearly 32,000 Arby’s, Baskin-Robbins, Buffalo Wild Wings, Dunkin’, Jimmy John’s and SONIC Drive-In restaurants worldwide.We’re made up of some of the world’s most iconic restaurant brands, but we’re much more than just a restaurant company. We know that food is much more than a staple—it’s an experience. At Inspire, that’s our purpose: to ignite and nourish flavorful experiences.
